zai-org/GLM-4.5-Air_1.Option: The resistance in M. fahalii is due to a structural alteration in MFCYP51A2 that impairs optimal drug-target coordination despite similar overall binding energy, explaining its ability to grow at high itraconazole concentrations.

This structural alteration impairs the formation of the inhibitory Fe-N coordination bond, explaining the resistance mechanism. The option correctly interprets these findings by concluding that resistance arises from impaired drug-target coordination rather than reduced binding affinity. The passage supports this conclusion, noting that the Fe-N distance is critical for azole inhibition and that MFCYP51A2 exhibits a weak interaction with itraconazole despite similar binding energies.
